chunks.text: Form No HCJDC-l2I ORDERSHEEI IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT LAHORE JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT WP No. 8244 0f 20I9 Syed Sajjad Hussain Judge Family Court, ete S.No. 0f Date of Order with signature of Judge and that of parties or Order order counse where necessary Proceeding proceeding 13.02.2019 Mr Ijaz Ahmad Janjua, Advocate for petitioner 1s Zarish Fatima, Assistant Attomey General and Mr Muhammad Javaid Iqbal Sabri, AAG. On Coun' call Ms. Nasrullah Khan Babar, Ch Muhammad Naseer and Ms Uuna Razzaq han Advocates Amicus curiae. Through this constitutional petition, the petitioner has called in question the judgment and decree dared 26.10.2018 passed by leared Judge Family Court Ferozewala whereby the suit for recovery of dower filed by respondent No Z wife (respondent ) was decreed in her faVOur Whereas the suit for restitution of conjugal rights filed by the petitioner was dismissed Brief facts of the case are that marriage between the parties WaS solemnized 0n 10.07.2011 in lieu of defered dower amounuing Rs.1,00,000/- Subsequently respondent No2 claiming to have been divorced by the petitioner in June-2Ol4 filed suit for recovery of aforesaid deferred dower The petitioner انامن 7٢ ٦٦ ٧٥٦ denied the facts through contesting written statement also filed suit for restitution of conjugal rights Both the afore refered suits were consolidated and the leamed trial count vide its judgment and decree dated 26.10.2018 decreed the suit filed by the respondent for recovery of Rs O,U0O/- as defered dower whereas the petitioner 8 suit W٥3 dismissed Feeling aggrieved the said judgment and decree, the petitioner filed appeal before the appellate court, which vide order dated 07.12,2018 declared the appeal to the extent of recovery of dowry articles as not maintainable in view of bar provided under section of the Family Courts Act 1964 ('4ct'), however has entertained the appeal the dismissal of petitioner' suit for dissolution marriage The petitioner has flled this constitutional petition the decree for recovery of dower passed by the trial court Leamed counsel for the petitioner has argued that petitioner has not divorced the respondent, therefore, she could n0t claim deferred dower during the subsistence of marriage; that the petitioner has elready paid defened dower to the respondent, hence the leamed trial count has ered in law while decreeing the suit filed said against challenge

Nasrullah Khan Babar, Ch Muhammad Naseer and Ms Uuna Razzaq han Advocates Amicus curiae. Through this constitutional petition, the petitioner has called in question the judgment and decree dared 26.10.2018 passed by leared Judge Family Court Ferozewala whereby the suit for recovery of dower filed by respondent No Z wife (respondent ) was decreed in her faVOur Whereas the suit for restitution of conjugal rights filed by the petitioner was dismissed Brief facts of the case are that marriage between the parties WaS solemnized 0n 10.07.2011 in lieu of defered dower amounuing Rs.1,00,000/- Subsequently respondent No2 claiming to have been divorced by the petitioner in June-2Ol4 filed suit for recovery of aforesaid deferred dower The petitioner انامن 7٢ ٦٦ ٧٥٦ denied the facts through contesting written statement also filed suit for restitution of conjugal rights Both the afore refered suits were consolidated and the leamed trial count vide its judgment and decree dated 26.10.2018 decreed the suit filed by the respondent for recovery of Rs O,U0O/- as defered dower whereas the petitioner 8 suit W٥3 dismissed Feeling aggrieved the said judgment and decree, the petitioner filed appeal before the appellate court, which vide order dated 07.12,2018 declared the appeal to the extent of recovery of dowry articles as not maintainable in view of bar provided under section of the Family Courts Act 1964 ('4ct'), however has entertained the appeal the dismissal of petitioner' suit for dissolution marriage The petitioner has flled this constitutional petition the decree for recovery of dower passed by the trial court Leamed counsel for the petitioner has argued that petitioner has not divorced the respondent, therefore, she could n0t claim deferred dower during the subsistence of marriage; that the petitioner has elready paid defened dower to the respondent, hence the leamed trial count has ered in law while decreeing the suit filed said against challenge چجچده ٧٦ ٥٢٦ ذ371٦11!٥5 by the respondent by assuming that marriage had been dissolved Learned Law Officers well as amicus curIa٥ appointed this Count have argued that without comm enting upon the fact that whether the marriage had actually been dissolved or not, as the petitioner claims to have paid dower to the respondent during the subsistence marriage, therefore the contention of the petitioner that defered dower could n0t claimed the respondent before dissolution of marriage would be without justification in the given circumstances of the case Heard Record perused The main question to be determined by this Court whether in the given circumstances of the case in view of the petitioner s claim that dissolution of marriage has not been established the record through any cogent evidence, the court had rightly decreed the respondent claim for payment of deferred dower It is an admitted fact that respondent Was married to the petitioner on 1OD7 2OI1 in lieu of deferred dower amounting to RsIOODOO/ and from the said wed lock two minor children were bom The respondent claims ٦1٨ ٠t 0٦4 ٧0ئا شaة [nat the petitioner had divorced him 2014 therefore is liable Pa} defered dower The petitioner denies the said facts and claims to have already dower to the respondent in Iw0 installments of Rs S0,OOO/- each in the month of February and May-2012 and nothing is due and outstanding against him On the one hand the petitioner claims that payment of defened dower has not become due as marriage is still intact and on the other hand claims to have the same during the subsistence of marriage Both the afore referred positions cannot ٥0-0x15t ٥٨٥ the pleas the petitioner contradictory and self destructive Defered dower usually n0[ payable immediately tne time performance of marriage ceremony Nikah as required in case of prompt dower and 1s referred to ٥3 deferred because iIs payment if reserved to be made Iater however, the codified law does not prescribe when the deiered dower becomes payable and the law has left the same to be decided by the themselves Deferred dower becomes to the wife on the fixed expiry of time period on the occurrence of any event r fulfilment of pre condition fixed for payment of the same in the Nikahnama or otherwise and ifneither such date or June, paid paid parties payable date,

Nasrullah Khan Babar, Ch Muhammad Naseer and Ms Uuna Razzaq han Advocates Amicus curiae. Through this constitutional petition, the petitioner has called in question the judgment and decree dared 26.10.2018 passed by leared Judge Family Court Ferozewala whereby the suit for recovery of dower filed by respondent No Z wife (respondent ) was decreed in her faVOur Whereas the suit for restitution of conjugal rights filed by the petitioner was dismissed Brief facts of the case are that marriage between the parties WaS solemnized 0n 10.07.2011 in lieu of defered dower amounuing Rs.1,00,000/- Subsequently respondent No2 claiming to have been divorced by the petitioner in June-2Ol4 filed suit for recovery of aforesaid deferred dower The petitioner انامن 7٢ ٦٦ ٧٥٦ denied the facts through contesting written statement also filed suit for restitution of conjugal rights Both the afore refered suits were consolidated and the leamed trial count vide its judgment and decree dated 26.10.2018 decreed the suit filed by the respondent for recovery of Rs O,U0O/- as defered dower whereas the petitioner 8 suit W٥3 dismissed Feeling aggrieved the said judgment and decree, the petitioner filed appeal before the appellate court, which vide order dated 07.12,2018 declared the appeal to the extent of recovery of dowry articles as not maintainable in view of bar provided under section of the Family Courts Act 1964 ('4ct'), however has entertained the appeal the dismissal of petitioner' suit for dissolution marriage The petitioner has flled this constitutional petition the decree for recovery of dower passed by the trial court Leamed counsel for the petitioner has argued that petitioner has not divorced the respondent, therefore, she could n0t claim deferred dower during the subsistence of marriage; that the petitioner has elready paid defened dower to the respondent, hence the leamed trial count has ered in law while decreeing the suit filed said against challenge چجچده ٧٦ ٥٢٦ ذ371٦11!٥5 by the respondent by assuming that marriage had been dissolved Learned Law Officers well as amicus curIa٥ appointed this Count have argued that without comm enting upon the fact that whether the marriage had actually been dissolved or not, as the petitioner claims to have paid dower to the respondent during the subsistence marriage, therefore the contention of the petitioner that defered dower could n0t claimed the respondent before dissolution of marriage would be without justification in the given circumstances of the case Heard Record perused The main question to be determined by this Court whether in the given circumstances of the case in view of the petitioner s claim that dissolution of marriage has not been established the record through any cogent evidence, the court had rightly decreed the respondent claim for payment of deferred dower It is an admitted fact that respondent Was married to the petitioner on 1OD7 2OI1 in lieu of deferred dower amounting to RsIOODOO/ and from the said wed lock two minor children were bom The respondent claims ٦1٨ ٠t 0٦4 ٧0ئا شaة [nat the petitioner had divorced him 2014 therefore is liable Pa} defered dower The petitioner denies the said facts and claims to have already dower to the respondent in Iw0 installments of Rs S0,OOO/- each in the month of February and May-2012 and nothing is due and outstanding against him On the one hand the petitioner claims that payment of defened dower has not become due as marriage is still intact and on the other hand claims to have the same during the subsistence of marriage Both the afore referred positions cannot ٥0-0x15t ٥٨٥ the pleas the petitioner contradictory and self destructive Defered dower usually n0[ payable immediately tne time performance of marriage ceremony Nikah as required in case of prompt dower and 1s referred to ٥3 deferred because iIs payment if reserved to be made Iater however, the codified law does not prescribe when the deiered dower becomes payable and the law has left the same to be decided by the themselves Deferred dower becomes to the wife on the fixed expiry of time period on the occurrence of any event r fulfilment of pre condition fixed for payment of the same in the Nikahnama or otherwise and ifneither such date or June, paid paid parties payable date, ٧٦ ٢٥ و وe كاه period is fixed nor any condition is imposed, the same becomes payable on the dissolution of marriage by death Or divorce Reliance in this behalf is placed on tne judgment reported as Sففdع فف مفسا فoظ تفش 2009( عسعف فه مععفتعه تعآ لشسا SCMR I458), wherein it is observed as undera: F ts clear Jfrom the passages Jusا quoted from the book niled Kiiab-al Fiqh 4l-al- Madhahab-al- arba'a Ihar ع1[ Hanaj ]41515 4ll٥we bofh caiegories Mahr Mu 'ajjal |PFONPI immediately paymenr) an٥ Mu wajjal (dejerred L8 payment qjler certain time) However Whera Part Ih٤ d0w8r described Mu wajjal ie dejerred bur no time dimn Jued J٥r If5 * Payment accoraing Io 50I18 Ju!5t5, ١he condillon i5 valid and time of the dejerred paymenr Is elther aealh or atvorce In the present case although in the Nikahnama no such date, time period or condition for payment has fixed, however, the petitioner claims to have paid the same in the year 2012 Non fixation of any condition date ٥r time for payment of deferred dower in Nikahnama does n0[ preclude tne parties subsequently fixing the same through consent or conduct Even otherwise, in the absence of any agreed stipulation Iatur, been period relating t0 time of payment of deferred dower, husband who has to make payment of the same, can bilaterally or unilaterally, expressly or impliedly through his conduct waive the condition of waiting till the dissolution of marriage for making such payment by tendering dower; or agreeing tender the same immediately or on future date, expiry of some time period or on the happening of some event or fulfilment of fixed condition which purpose subsistence dissolution of marriage would be inelevant Therefore Without commenting upon the fact of subsistence dissolution of Iest may prejudice the appeal filed by the petitioner against the dismissal ofhis suit for restitution of conjugal rights, cOUI 0n the basis of petitioner = plea that dower had already been would be Justified to presume Uhat the through their conduct had modified the condition for payment of dower on the dates in February May-2012 when the petitioner claims have the same the respondent's demand instead waiting for the dissolution of marriage through divorce death.

2012 Non fixation of any condition date ٥r time for payment of deferred dower in Nikahnama does n0[ preclude tne parties subsequently fixing the same through consent or conduct Even otherwise, in the through his conduct waive the condition of waiting till the dissolution of marriage for making such payment by tendering dower; or agreeing tender the same immediately or on future date, expiry of some time period or on the happening of some event or fulfilment of fixed condition which purpose subsistence dissolution of marriage would be inelevant Therefore Without commenting upon the fact of subsistence dissolution of Iest may prejudice the appeal filed by the petitioner against the dismissal ofhis suit for restitution of conjugal rights, cOUI 0n the basis of petitioner = plea that dower had already been would be Justified to presume Uhat the through their conduct had modified the condition for payment of dower on the dates in February May-2012 when the petitioner claims have the same the respondent's demand instead waiting for the dissolution of marriage through divorce death. Although all presumptions are rebuttable, unless treated conclusive proof by law, the petitioner Was required to marriage paid parties 4nd paid Imiiiiia show through some cogent and confidence inspiring evidence that either he had not agreed to pay the deferred dower in the months of February and May-2OI2 or after agreed to pay the same he had actually made the said payment As the petitioner claims have paid dower on the asking of the respondent and that too during the subsistence of marriage, therefore, at this stage he is estopped by his conduct to claim that deferred dower Was not payable the subsistence of and view of the aforesaid position taken by him he had to establish the fact of such payment through cogent evidence which has not been done as the petitioner 8 own witness Syed Munawar Hussain Shah, who appeared in his support as DW٠2, has although deposed that dower had been but his statement is sketchy and he did not SIAt Uhat he Was witness to the said payment, hence, the payment dower by the petitioner has not been established the record The trial coun such circumstances was justified to decree the suit filed by the respondent for recovery of deferred dower and the said cannot be reversed regardless of the fact that the dissolution of marriage has been established or not having during marlage paid finding For the reasons recorded above, this constitutional petition devoid ofany force is dismtssed (MUZAMIL AKHTAR SHABIR) JUDGE 4DششLفd لتفعفتش& Nolyad being
